title: eriophyllum lanatum var. obovatum
intro: Eriophyllum lanatum var. obovatum, also known as Woolly Sunflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Woolly Sunflower include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, as well as its ability to act as a natural antihistamine and anti-allergenic agent. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a dried or fresh herb in teas, tinctures, and infusions, with the leaves and flowers being the most commonly used parts for medicinal purposes.
